name: MetaTrader 5 MCP Server
what_it_does: MetaTrader 5 MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with MetaTrader 5 — the industry-standard trading platform — accessing real-time and historical market data, querying account positions, analyzing technical indicators, and integrating MT5's comprehensive trading environment into agent-driven financial analysis workflows.
use_cases: Accessing real-time forex and CFD price data from market analysis agents
Querying account positions and portfolio status from portfolio management agents
Retrieving historical OHLCV data for backtesting from trading strategy agents
Monitoring open trades and profit/loss from risk management agents
Analyzing technical indicators via MT5's built-in functions from analysis agents
Automating trade entry via Expert Advisor integration from trading agents
not_for: Teams without MetaTrader 5 account and broker connection
Teams needing stock market data (MT5 focuses on forex/CFDs)
best_when: A forex or CFD trader uses MetaTrader 5 and wants AI agents to analyze market data, monitor positions, and assist with trading decisions — connecting AI intelligence to MT5's market environment.
avoid_when: You don't use MT5 or trade forex/CFDs. For crypto, use crypto-specific MCPs.

auth.notes: No MCP-level authentication — connects to local MetaTrader 5 terminal via Python MT5 library. MT5 must be running and logged in with broker account.

pricing.free_tier_limits: MetaTrader 5: free platform; requires funded broker account for live trading
pricing.paid_tiers: Broker spreads/commissions apply for live trading
pricing.notes: MT5 platform is free. Demo accounts available. Community MCP is free. Live trading requires broker account.

performance.rate_limits: Local MT5 terminal — limited by broker's data feed speed

known_agent_gotchas: MetaTrader 5 must be running and connected to broker — local terminal required
Python MetaTrader5 library must be installed (Windows-only)
Live trading via AI agents carries significant financial risk — test with demo account
Broker's data feed quality affects data accuracy
Community MCP from individual contributor — experimental quality
MT5 Python API is Windows-only — not available on macOS/Linux
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local MT5 terminal access. No external credentials for MCP. Financial risk: only enable order placement on explicitly authorized agents. Windows only.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local MT5 access. No credentials for MCP. High financial risk for order placement. Demo test first.
